programs.name: MassHealth Medicare Savings Program
programs.what_it_does: This program (formerly MassHealth Senior Buy-In) helps pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and co-insurance for low-income seniors. It includes QMB, SLMB, and QI equivalents to reduce out-of-pocket Medicare costs.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+ or disabled, Massachusetts residents meeting specific income limits (varies by type).[5]
programs.benefit_value: Pays Medicare premiums (e.g., Part B $174.70/month in 2026) and cost-sharing; exact value based on income bracket.[5]

programs.name: Frail Elder Waiver (FEW)
programs.what_it_does: The Frail Elder Home & Community-Based Services Waiver provides supports at home, adult foster care, or congregate housing for nursing home-qualified seniors. Services include personal care, homemaker help, home modifications, meal delivery, and emergency response systems.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home-qualified frail elders aged 65+, including those with Alzheimer's or related dementias, meeting MassHealth income/asset eligibility.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; covers cost of home-based alternatives to institutional care, potentially saving thousands in nursing home expenses.[1]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE combines Medicaid and Medicare benefits into one program offering comprehensive care, including long-term services and additional benefits like dental and eye care. It enables frail seniors to live in the community with coordinated medical and social supports.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Dual-eligible (Medicare and Medicaid) frail seniors aged 55+ certified as nursing facility level of care, residing in a PACE service area.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full integration of Medicare/Medicaid benefits plus extras like dental/eye care; no additional premiums for enrollees.[1]

programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy food for eligible low-income households. In Massachusetts, administered as part of DTA benefits for seniors.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ with income up to 200% FPL (higher disregard for elderly); Massachusetts residents.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household; max benefit scales with household size and income.

programs.name: Fuel Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income households with heating costs through cash grants or vendor payments during heating season. Administered by Mass DTA and local agencies.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Households with income up to 60% state median (about $45,000 for one in 2026); prioritizes seniors 60+ and disabled.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Average grant $600-1,000 per heating season (Oct-Apr); up to $2,000 max based on fuel type and need.

programs.name: Residential Conservation Service (Weatherization)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce energy costs. Part of federal Weatherization Assistance Program via MassSave.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (up to 200% FPL), prioritizing seniors, disabled, and families with children; MA residents.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Free services worth $5,000-10,000 per home; lifetime energy savings of 20-30% on bills.

programs.name: SHINE (Serving Health Information Needs of Elders)
programs.what_it_does: Free counseling on Medicare, MassHealth, and prescription drug plans by trained volunteers. Helps seniors choose and enroll in optimal coverage.[3]
programs.who_its_for: All Massachusetts Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, and families seeking health insurance guidance.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling; potential savings of hundreds to thousands annually on premiums/drugs.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Coordinated through Councils on Aging and Nutrition Project.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or with mobility issues.[1]

programs.name: Alzheimer’s Respite Care Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite services like in-home aid or adult day care to relieve family caregivers of Alzheimer’s/dementia patients. Part of MassHealth home care supports.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of MA seniors with Alzheimer’s or related dementias meeting MassHealth eligibility.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $2,000/year per caregiver in services; varies by assessed need.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service work training for low-income seniors to gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment. Administered by MA agencies.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income individuals 55+ (income ≤125% FPL) in MA; priority for veterans, disabled.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/wk, ~$10,000/year); plus training and job placement.

programs.name: Massachusetts Senior Legal Helpline
programs.what_it_does: Free legal advice and representation for income-eligible seniors on issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and consumer problems. Operated by Greater Boston Legal Services.[3]
programs.who_its_for: MA residents 60+ with income ≤125% FPL (~$18,000 individual); 200% for housing cases.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services worth thousands; prevents evictions, secures benefits.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_dos: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities to resolve complaints and protect rights. Statewide network investigates issues and mediates.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Residents, families, and staff in MA long-term care facilities; open to public inquiries.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving ~80% complaints without litigation; protects rights and improves care quality.

programs.name: Prescription Advantage
programs.what_it_does: Provides prescription drug coverage to eligible Massachusetts residents. It helps cover costs for medications not fully paid by Medicare or other insurance.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts residents age 65 and older, and younger individuals with disabilities who meet income and employment guidelines.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical assistance program specific to Massachusetts, supplementing federal Medicare coverage for low-income seniors.
programs.benefit_value: Reduces out-of-pocket prescription costs based on income; exact amounts vary by eligibility and drug costs.

programs.name: Home Modification and Loan Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers low-interest loans for home modifications to support aging in place. Covers installations like ramps, stair lifts, and bathroom adaptations.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts homeowners needing accessibility modifications, typically seniors or people with disabilities.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific 0% to 3% interest loans up to $30,000 administered by the Massachusetts Rehabilitation Commission, beyond standard federal programs.

programs.name: Home Care Program (HCP)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers in-home care management and support services to facilitate aging in place. Includes homemaker services, personal care, and meals.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts seniors, people with disabilities, and those with early-stage dementia able to complete some ADLs, determined by age, residence, and needs.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded through Aging Services Access Points (ASAPs) with comprehensive interdisciplinary assessments, distinct from federal HCBS waivers.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by needs assessment; services like personal care, transportation, meals on sliding-scale co-pays.

programs.name: Enhanced Community Options Program (ECOP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides enhanced in-home and non-medical assistance for those needing more intensive care than HCP. Develops care plans with social workers for family caregivers.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts seniors requiring greater degree of care than standard home care, assessed via ADLs.
programs.whats_unique: Massachusetts-specific program for higher-needs elders, administered through ASAPs with sliding-scale fees, not available nationwide.
programs.benefit_value: Expanded services including higher-level personal care and support; co-pays on sliding scale.

programs.name: Senior Care Options (SCO)
programs.what_it_does: Integrates Medicare and MassHealth benefits into one health plan with added support services. Includes care coordination for health and social needs.
programs.who_its_for: Dual-eligible seniors age 65+ enrolled in both Medicare and MassHealth Standard, in select MA counties.
programs.whats_unique: Massachusetts-specific integrated care model for dual-eligibles, offering extras like one care coordinator beyond standard federal dual plans.
programs.benefit_value: All Medicare/MassHealth benefits at no cost plus extras like expanded support services.
